{
  "decision": "fail",

  "component_conditions": [

  "percent_complete": 90,

  "confidence": 1.0,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Standard expanding foam used where fire-rated sealant is required",
    "Non-compliant fireblocking material at penetrations"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the non-compliant foam sealant from the penetrations",
    "Clean the penetration joints of all foam residue",
    "Apply approved fire-rated sealant/firestopping material complying with ASTM E136 or ASTM E814"
  ],
  "rationale": "The installation of standard expanding foam instead of an approved fire-rated sealant violates IRC R302 fireblocking requirements. Fireblocking materials must securely fill all penetrations to prevent the passage of flame and hot gases, and standard foam does not provide the required fire-resistance rating.",
  "references": [
    "IRC R302.11",
    "IRC R302.11.1"
  ]
}
